title: Perfectionism and Its Emotional Cost
description: Perfectionism involves holding oneself to standards that leave little room for error, often tying self-worth tightly to achievement or flawless performance. It can develop as a way to earn approval, avoid criticism, or feel a sense of control. While it may drive real accomplishment, it frequently comes with chronic dissatisfaction, fear of failure, and difficulty recognizing or enjoying success.
observation_language: It seems like there’s a pattern of holding yourself to very high standards, even when it takes a toll on you.
There appears to be a tendency to focus on what wasn’t good enough rather than what went well.
It looks like it can be hard to feel satisfied or finished with something, even after a lot of effort.
There seems to be a pattern of tying your sense of worth to how well you perform.
underlying_fears: Fear of not being good enough
Fear of being judged or criticized
